patch.value: Harden secret-scan guardrail: detect all GitHub token prefixes, make rules unit-testable, redact [redacted_local_ops_detail] from gitignored ops doc

A privacy re-audit (sub-agent sweep of tracked files, the .agents/pm history streams, and gitignored scripts/prod/telemetry) confirmed the public shipping surface (git-tracked files + npm tarball + all 776 history streams) is CLEAN. Two gaps surfaced: (1) scripts/check-secrets.mjs only matched ghp_/github_pat_ GitHub tokens, missing gho_/ghu_/ghs_/ghr_ (OAuth/user/server/refresh) prefixes; (2) the scanner ran on import with no exported rules, so detection logic had no unit coverage. Separately, a gitignored local ops doc ([redacted_local_ops_detail]) stored a [redacted_local_ops_detail] — it never ships (gitignored) but should not sit in plaintext.

Secret scanner detects all GitHub token prefixes (ghp_/gho_/ghu_/ghs_/ghr_/github_pat_); RULES + scanContent are exported and covered by a unit test; the scanner only auto-runs when invoked as a CLI (no side effect on import); the [redacted_local_ops_detail] is removed from the gitignored ops doc; pnpm security:scan still passes with zero findings on tracked files; coverage stays 100%.

value.note: broaden github-token rule to gh[opsru]_; export RULES+scanContent; guard run() to CLI-only invocation
regression: all GitHub token prefixes + excludeFiles + clean-content negative cases

patch.value: Broadened the github-token rule in scripts/check-secrets.mjs from ghp_/github_pat_ to gh[opsru]_ (ghp/gho/ghu/ghs/ghr) + github_pat_. Refactored the scanner to export RULES and scanContent(file, content) and guarded run() behind a CLI-entry check (fileURLToPath(import.meta.url) === process.argv[1]) so importing it has no side effects. Added tests/unit/check-secrets.spec.ts covering all token prefixes, other high-risk shapes (private-key/aws/sentry-org/ssh-target), excludeFiles behavior, and clean-content negatives. Redacted the [redacted_local_ops_detail] from the gitignored [redacted_local_ops_detail] (replaced with a pointer to read from [redacted_local_ops_detail] / .env [redacted_local_ops_detail]); confirmed the literal exists nowhere else. pnpm security:scan passes 'No credential-like secrets detected in tracked files.'; new spec passes 5/5. scripts/ is excluded from the coverage allowlist so 100% coverage is unaffected.
Secret-scan guardrail now covers all GitHub token prefixes and is unit-tested; [redacted_local_ops_detail] removed from the local ops doc.
CI secret gate catches a wider class of GitHub tokens and the detection rules are regression-protected; no plaintext credential remains on disk.
gho_/ghu_/ghs_/ghr_ now detected; RULES/scanContent exported and covered (5/5); ops doc password redacted; security:scan clean.

Secret scanner detects all GitHub token prefixes (ghp_/gho_/ghu_/ghs_/ghr_/github_pat_); RULES + scanContent are exported and covered by a unit test; the scanner only auto-runs when invoked as a CLI; local-only credential material is removed from ignored operations notes; tracked secret scan remains clean; coverage stays 100%.

A privacy re-audit confirmed the public shipping surface is clean and identified scanner coverage gaps for additional GitHub token prefixes plus a local-only ignored operations note that needed credential hygiene. The implementation broadened scanner coverage, made the rules unit-testable, and removed the local plaintext credential reference without tracking private operations details.
